html:
Annexure A
Research Endowment Fund
RESEARCH APPLICATION FORM
GRANT APPLIED FOR {{ doc.project_period or "July 2025 — June 2026" }}
1. RESEARCH TITLE

{{ doc.research_title or "Strengthening Academia-Policymaker Engagement for More Effective Evidence-based Social Development" }}

2.2 Researchers' Experience and Capabilities
Briefly explain the experience of PI and Co-PI(s) in relation to the proposed project (100-150 words)

{{ doc.researchers_experience or "The experience and expertise of the PI and CO-PIs are well aligned with the project needs and research focus. The project will further enhance their role as academics. Dr. Chencho Lhamu, Dean of External Relations and Development at Royal Thimphu College (RTC) and Principal Investigator (PI), brings expertise in school and tertiary education, education reform initiatives, and civil society engagement. Dr. Tshering Dolkar, Professor at the Faculty of Social Sciences in RTC, serving as Co-PI, also brings expertise in school and tertiary education as well as experience in policy planning and monitoring. Dr. Rachel Jacob, Professor at the Faculty of Humanities in RTC and Co-PI, brings expertise in media policy and research analysis." }}

RESEARCH PROJECT DESCRIPTION
1. Abstract
Provide abstract (250-300 words)

{{ doc.abstract or "There is a growing global consensus for universities to expand their roles beyond the two missions of teaching and research and embrace the 'Third Mission' of contributing to society (Compagnucci & Spigarelli, 2020). Universities have been moving away from traditional 'ivory towers' towards engaging with society to contribute to social development. However, the extent of this trend in Bhutan remains to be investigated. Current interactions between academia and policymakers seem limited to enforcement, regulation, or funding contexts; and while research evidence is available in Higher Education Institutes (HEIs), its application in public policy is unclear. This disconnect in Bhutan is evident in concerns over fragmented governance in the civil service, as indicated in His Majesty's Kasho (Kuensel, 2021) and the Asian Development Bank (ADB)'s technical support for 'more informed policymaking' (ADB, 2020). This study—the first of its kind in Bhutan—seeks to strengthen this engagement, advancing evidence-based policymaking for inclusive and equitable social development." }}

2. Background and Problem Statement
Provide a brief background (250-300 words)

{{ doc.bg_and_prb_statement or "There is a growing global consensus for universities to expand their roles beyond the two missions of teaching and research and embrace the 'Third Mission' of contributing to society (Compagnucci & Spigarelli, 2020). Universities have been moving away from traditional 'ivory towers' towards engaging with society to contribute to social development. However, the extent of this trend in Bhutan remains to be investigated. Current interactions between academia and policymakers seem limited to enforcement, regulation, or funding contexts; and while research evidence is available in Higher Education Institutes (HEIs), its application in public policy is unclear. This disconnect in Bhutan is evident in concerns over fragmented governance in the civil service, as indicated in His Majesty's Kasho (Kuensel, 2021) and the Asian Development Bank (ADB)'s technical support for 'more informed policymaking' (ADB, 2020). This study—the first of its kind in Bhutan—seeks to strengthen this engagement, advancing evidence-based policymaking for inclusive and equitable social development." }}

3. Research Aim and Objectives
Clearly state the aim and objectives (50-100 words)

Objectives: {{ doc.research_objectives or "This research explores the gaps between HEI researchers and policymakers in Bhutan, aiming to identify factors influencing their engagement and strategies to enhance the integration of HEI research into policy for more effective social development." }}

4. Research Questions or Hypotheses
List specific research questions (50-100 words)

Key question: {{ doc.res_q_or_hypo or "How can HEI research integration into policy be strengthened for more effective evidence-based social development?" }}

5. Literature Review
Summarize key literature (400-500 words)

{{ doc.literature_review or "Since the 1980s, universities worldwide have evolved from focusing primarily on education and research to embracing the 'Third Mission' (TM), which fosters societal contributions (Compagnucci & Spigarelli, 2020). Broadly defined as 'engagement' (Cunha & Benneworth, 2013), TM serves as a transmission mechanism for assessing academia's impact on society and has gained increasing scholarly interest. Other scholarly works have identified organizational and socioeconomic factors that influence researcher-policymaker engagement and the uptake of research (Sasse & Haddon, 2018; Stone, 2002; Strydom et al., 2010; Tseng, 2012). Stone (2002), for instance, highlights challenges on both the 'supply' (researchers) and 'demand' (policymakers) sides, building on the 'two-communities' theory. Despite growing research on academia-policymaker engagement, much of the literature is Western-centric and may not fully apply to Bhutan's unique Gross National Happiness (GNH) development model." }}

6. Methodology
Describe study design, methods, analysis (1000 words)

6.1 Research Design: {{ doc.research_design or "The study will adopt an exploratory Sequential Design to provide a nuanced understanding of the challenges and opportunities for strengthening researcher-policymaker engagement and enhancing research uptake. The research conceptualises the research-policy nexus as a dynamic, two-way interactive process. The uptake of research is influenced by both challenges and enabling factors that arise from both the supply (researchers) and the demand side (policy and program personnel). By combining qualitative insights, network analysis, and participatory ideation, this study aims to offer a nuanced understanding of the research-policy interface and practical pathways for strengthening evidence-based policymaking." }}

6.2 Sampling Design: {{doc.sampling_design}}

6.3 Data Collection Tools and Procedures: {{doc.data_collection_tools_and_procedures}}

6.4 Data Analysis Tools and Procedures: {{doc.data_analysis_tools_and_procedures}}

6.5 Data Presentation {{doc.data_presentation}}

7. Ethical Consideration
Statement on the ethical conduct of the research

7.1. Statement on health and safety considerations: {{doc.statement_on_health_and_safety}}

7.2 Subject research: {{doc.subject_research}}

7.3. Other animal research: {{doc.other_animal_research}}

7.4 Protection of Environment: {{doc.protection_of_environment}}

8. Statement on potential conflict of interest
Full disclosure of any potential conflicts of interest and plan to manage them

{{doc.statement_on_potential_conflict_of_interest}}

{{doc.conflict_of_interest_attachment}}

9. Collaborations
Describe required collaborations (50 words)

{{ doc.role_of_collaborators or "Policymakers, HEI researchers, parliamentarians, program personnel, and intermediaries will provide insights through surveys and KIIs. Ideation workshops will facilitate co-creation, identifying challenges and solutions while also validating findings and refining recommendations, fostering collective ownership. This collaborative approach strengthens research-policy linkages for informed decision-making." }}

10. Data Dissemination Plan
Describe dissemination strategy (50-100 words)

{{ doc.data_dissemination_plan or "Using a participatory approach, the researchers will involve stakeholders in co-developing recommendations and solutions—intentionally designed to aid dissemination. Our strategy will be stakeholder-centred, leveraging multiple platforms and formats for accessibility and engagement. We will host policy dialogues and webinars for all stakeholders. Concise policy briefs/executive summaries will be tailored for policymakers, while infographics, explainer videos, and simplified social media content will target civil society, media and the general public." }}

11. Research Impact
Expected outcomes and contributions (50-100 words)

{{ doc.research_impact or "This study will generate valuable insights into the engagement between HEI researchers and policymakers in Bhutan, shedding light on existing gaps, barriers, and opportunities for collaboration. By examining how research evidence is integrated into policy and programmatic decisions, it will contribute to strategies that enhance knowledge exchange and improve the societal relevance of academic research. Ultimately, this research aims to strengthen evidence-based policymaking, leading to more informed and socially impactful policies and programmes that drive sustainable social development in Bhutan." }}

12. Intellectual Property
Mention if the project has research outcomes

{{doc.intellectual_property}}

13. References
Follow APA referencing style

{{doc.references}}

14. Budget

Total Budget: {{ doc.currency or "Nu." }} {{ doc.total_expenses or "4,47,900" }}

11. Ethical Considerations
Explain ethical considerations (50-100 words)

{{ doc.ethical_considerations or "The research team will adhere to ethical clearance procedures as outlined in the Royal University of Bhutan (RUB) research policies (RUB, 2014). Upon confirmation of the research project with REF support, the team will ensure informed consent from participants, emphasizing 'their right to withdraw at any time without reason' (Fleming & Zegwaard, 2018, p. 211). We will provide them with comprehensive information about the study including its significance and details on the protection of their identity and confidentiality." }}

Certification

This is to certify that the details provided in this application form are true and complete, and evaluated and endorsed by the College.

Chair, College Research Committee

Endorsement by the President of College

I certify that the research/project can be accommodated within the general understanding of the College in relation to workloads, leave availability, and overall research facilities.

Signature: ____________________

Name: {{ doc.president_name or "Shiva Raj Bhattarai" }}
Date: {{ doc.president_date or frappe.utils.formatdate(frappe.utils.nowdate()) }}

Note: Please submit this word version and the scanned application form through DRIL to DeAR, OVC

[^1] There are 12 colleges affiliated with the Royal University of Bhutan (RUB), established in 2003. Two autonomous tertiary institutes and 3 faculties are associated with the Khesar Gyalpo University of Medical Sciences (KGUMS), instituted in 2015 (Gyeltshen & Dorji, 2020).
